steps.title: Identify where the y-intercept occurs
steps.content: The $y$-intercept of a graph is the point where the graph crosses the $y$-axis. Every point on the $y$-axis has $x=0$, so the intercept has the form $(0,y)$.

steps.title: Read the y-value from the graph
steps.content: On the graph, the exponential curve crosses the $y$-axis at the marked point $(0,3)$. Therefore the missing $y$-coordinate in $(0,y)$ is $$y=3.$$

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The question asks for the value of $y$, not the full ordered pair. The answer is 3.
